program.name: Cerritos on Wheels (COW)
program.description: Cerritos on Wheels (COW) provides public transportation to over 180 locations throughout the city, arriving at stops every 60 minutes during operating days. COW is funded by local Proposition A and C funds administered by LA Metro.

routes.name: Green Route
routes.description: Connects major commercial and civic destinations in central Cerritos

routes.name: Blue Route
routes.description: Links educational institutions and the Auto Square commercial area

routes.name: Red Route
routes.description: Serves recreation facilities and residential neighborhoods

challenges.name: First COW Ride
challenges.description: Take your first ride on Cerritos on Wheels and log the trip

challenges.name: Week Commuter
challenges.description: Use COW or walk/bike for 5 trips in one week instead of driving

challenges.name: Route Explorer
challenges.description: Ride all 3 COW routes at least once and document your experience

challenges.name: Carpool Organizer
challenges.description: Organize a carpool with 3+ classmates for school or an event

impactCalculator.description: Calculate the environmental and financial impact of shifting trips from car to transit, walking, or biking.

scenarioPresets.name: Student Daily Commute
scenarioPresets.description: Typical school commute, 5 days/week round trip

sources.name: City of Cerritos — Cerritos on Wheels (COW)
EPA — Greenhouse Gas Emissions from a Typical Passenger Vehicle
